mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, the labor-income shock reduces household spending expectations and raises recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 6.1 percent as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers discount rates and signals policy support for liquidity-sensitive equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 2.0 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS do not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than 3-month increases across rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units indicate a severe residential investment contraction that weakens construction income and bank collateral values, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 965.0 thousand units in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1000 episodes have nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and payroll employment identify an economy-wide production and income contraction, causing analysts to cut earnings forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 110.3034 and PAYEMS was 137473.0 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-and-PAYEMS contraction signal has no lower 20-day forward return than either single-series contraction signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation paired with a low federal funds rate signals negative real policy rates and constrained inflation credibility, pressuring equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 219.181 and FEDFUNDS was 2.0 percent in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPI-low-FEDFUNDS condition does not underperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales indicates a near-term consumption shock that immediately lowers revenue expectations for cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 381169.0 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly RSAFS changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with rising industrial production confirms that aggregate demand is translating into current output, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11740.3 and INDPRO was 110.3034 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1-and-INDPRO signal fails to produce above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment signals persistent job destruction and weaker wage income, reducing expected consumption and corporate revenues, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 137473.0 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month declines do not predict lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than PAYEMS 3-month increases after accounting for rolling-window estimation.
